channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force dubs MQ-9 the ‘MVP’ of Epic Fury as lawmakers press manned-unmanned future | Defense News
item.description: The remotely piloted MQ-9 Reaper was the workhorse in the U.S. air campaign against Iran, Air Force leaders told Congress on May 20.

item.title: 🌍 The Middle East War Will Reshape Africa’s Democratic Trajectory | Just Security
item.description: The economic fallout across Africa from the Iran war will have major impact on the well-being of African citizens and the prospects for democracy on the continent. The post The Middle East War Will Reshape Africa’s Democratic Trajectory appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: ⚛️ For homeland missile defense, think Golden Zones, not a Golden Dome | Atlantic Council
item.description: While the second Trump administration correctly diagnoses a more dangerous nuclear environment facing the country, a comprehensive defensive system is the wrong prescription. The risks of prompting adversary proliferation and stretching US resources thin are too high. The post…

item.title: 🌏 Golden Dome is the missile defense the US needs | Atlantic Council
item.description: The United States should pursue a comprehensive missile defense system to protect the homeland from advanced threats posed by China, Russia, and North Korea. Political will from the Trump administration and advances in technology make the idea of the long-discussed "dome" over…

item.title: 🏛️ Hurst nominated as full Pentagon comptroller | Breaking Defense
item.description: He has been ‘performing the duties’ since August 2025 and oversaw the crafting and rollout of the $1.5 billion defense request for 2027.

item.title: 🏛️ New Engines Key To Night Stalker MH-60M Black Hawk Upgrade Plans | The War Zone
item.description: The Army's Improved Turbine Engine Program has promised a major boost in performance for Black Hawk helicopters, but its future is murky. The post New Engines Key To Night Stalker MH-60M Black Hawk Upgrade Plans appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ First Defense Critical Infrastructure summit aims to develop repeatable playbook | Breaking Defense
item.description: During a table top exercise, the Army, along with 14 external partners, gamed out how to respond to a coordinated and simultaneous attack on an installation as units are deploying.

item.title: 🏴 Europe must address the ‘digital sovereignty triad’ | Atlantic Council
item.description: As part of its Tech Sovereignty Package, the EU is developing its own definition of digital sovereignty. But a fundamental question will likely remain: how can the bloc balance control over data, legal authority, and vendor independence while maintaining openness to global…

item.title: 🛡️ Donald Trump’s War on Tourism | Foreign Policy
item.description: Turned off by U.S. policies and border practices, foreign visitors are going elsewhere.

item.title: 📍 The Calls for a U.S. War on Cuba Are Getting Louder | Foreign Policy
item.description: Florida Republicans want Trump to seize indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro.

item.title: 🌏 Paving the Way for Hostile Coexistence? What North Korea’s Constitutional Revisions Mean for Inter-Korean Ties | 38 North
item.description: The text of North Korea’s (Democratic People’s Republic of Korea; DPRK) latest constitution ... The post Paving the Way for Hostile Coexistence? What North Korea’s Constitutional Revisions Mean for Inter-Korean Ties appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 🏛️ Netherlands eyes more JASSM weapons for F-35, discloses ASW frigate delivery delay | Breaking Defense
item.description: The move to open talks comes after The Hague and Washington signed a 2024 Letter of Offer and Acceptance to initially acquire the deep strike capability.

item.title: 📍 FEPA’s First Test: Protecting American Companies Returning to Venezuela | Just Security
item.description: If FEPA is enforced seriously, U.S. companies operating in Venezuela will be able to push back against bribe demands with the full weight of U.S. law behind them. The post FEPA’s First Test: Protecting American Companies Returning to Venezuela appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 A Mid-Life Crisis for Senate Intelligence? | Just Security
item.description: This week marks the 50th anniversary of the Senate Select Committee on Intelligence, prompting reflection on lessons learned and potential new directions. The post A Mid-Life Crisis for Senate Intelligence? appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 The Kadyrov question: Who rules Chechnya next? | Atlantic Council
item.description: The latest report in the Atlantic Council's Russia Tomorrow series asseses the four pathways that may await Chechnya after Kadyrov is no longer in power. The post The Kadyrov question: Who rules Chechnya next? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌏 Energy Sanctions Dashboard: October 2025 | Atlantic Council
item.description: The October 2025 edition of the Energy Sanctions Dashboard analyzed China’s central role in the market for sanctioned oil and the techniques Russia, Iran, and Venezuela use to evade sanctions on oil. The post Energy Sanctions Dashboard: October 2025 appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🏴 Sanctions Towards Russia Are Not a Strategy: Toward a More Coherent Statecraft | Just Security
item.description: Sanctions have become a weapon of lawfare: a contest over the rule of law, governance models and the integrity of global markets. But systemic corruption cannot be sanctioned. The post Sanctions Towards Russia Are Not a Strategy: Toward a More Coherent Statecraft appeared first…

item.title: 📍 How drones can navigate without GPS | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] Absolute positioning is critical in contested and denied environments.

item.title: 📍 Turkey to buy 100 one-way explosive naval drones for swarm attacks | Defense News
item.description: Turkey eyes an arsenal capable of taking out enemy ships with unmanned systems.

item.title: 🕵️ ‘Have to be very careful’: Special ops head calls for combat AI ‘reality check’ | Breaking Defense
item.description: Adm. Frank Bradley told the audience at SOF Week that before unleashing “violence,” humans must be in the loop.

item.title: 📍 Early Edition: May 21, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: U.S. CARIBBEAN AND PACIFIC OPERATIONS “We in the U.S. are offering to help you not only alleviate the current crisis, but…

item.title: 📍 Defense Business Brief: Hybrid sky drones; Amphibs; Mobile data centers and a bit more | Defense One
item.description: Click to read the full story at the source.

item.title: 🌏 China’s AI Governance Offensive Threatens U.S. Tech Leadership | War on the Rocks
item.description: China’s diplomats are on an “AI governance” offensive. At a May 5 United Nations meeting, China’s vice minister of science and technology championed China’s role in shaping U.N.-led frameworks that determine how the technology should be built and used. Just a week earlier, two…

item.title: 🌍 The Operational Case Against Israel’s Gaza Campaign | War on the Rocks
item.description: Gaza did not have to look the way it looks. That is not a moral claim. It is an operational one.Claims of necessity are invoked to explain the scale of civilian harm, but they founder in the face of operational logic. Supporters of Israeli methods argue that Gaza’s urban…

item.title: 🏴 Europe’s Dangerous Hunger Games for American Troops | War on the Rocks
item.description: Every time reports emerge about a potential reduction of U.S. forces or capabilities in Europe, the old continent falls into the same cycle of anxiety and panic. The same applies to announcements about delayed or suspended deployments, planned rotations, or broader force posture…

item.title: 📍 Fighting online youth radicalisation, Australia and India can teach each other | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Two democracies, very different in scale, are grappling with the same problem: how to protect young people from radicalisation through online gaming. After Australia’s worst terrorist attack, the Bondi Beach massacre in December, the federal ...

item.title: 📍 The Crumbling Pillars of Global Peace | Foreign Affairs
item.description: War, empire, and the forgotten power of the United Nations.

item.title: 🏴 Beyond the front line: Ukraine is deepening its drone wall | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Long-range Ukrainian strikes have dominated recent headlines, and for good reason: they are causing lasting damage. In April, Oleksandr Syrskyi, Ukraine’s commander-in-chief, said strikes on Russian military infrastructure had caused around $25.5 billion in damage. ...

item.title: 🏴 Philippine Supreme Court Rejects Fugitive Senator’s Challenge to ICC Arrest Warrant | The Diplomat
item.description: The decision opens the way for the arrest for the senator, who is wanted by the ICC for his role in leading former President Rodrigo Duterte's violence anti-drug campaign.

item.title: 📍 Indonesian President Announces Plan to Centralize Control of Key Commodity Exports | The Diplomat
item.description: The move is likely to unsettle international investors already concerned about the uncertain trajectory of economic policymaking under Prabowo.
